label: Subgenres
description: Specific structural frameworks, world-states, or narrative logics that operate within a genre.

tags.definition: The world is actively ending during the course of the narrative
Focuses on biotechnology, genetic engineering, and body modification as the dominant technological framework
Climate Fiction; structured around environmental collapse as the defining world-state
Bildungsroman; the structural transition from youth to adult identity as the central arc
Works with transgressive structure and niche or subcultural appeal; often formally experimental
High-tech, low-life; digital networks and urban decay as the defining aesthetic and social framework
Structured around the procedural journey of an investigator through a case
A world defined by oppressive or failed societal control as the structural baseline
Narrative constructed entirely from letters, diary entries, documents, or other written records
Massive scope with high political, cosmic, or civilizational stakes; often in a secondary world
Structured around intelligence operations, state secrets, and institutional betrayal
Multi-generational lineage and domestic history as the organizing spine
Grounded, near-future extrapolation focused on the next technological or social steps
Subjective, chaotic, and author-centric; the narrator's own consciousness shapes the structure
Atmospheric decay, ancestral secrets, the sublime, and dread encoded in setting and architecture
A pure logic puzzle; an impossible crime or situation that must be solved by reason alone
Exaggerated plot and heightened emotional stakes; moral polarity between characters
Episodic adventures of a roguish, low-status protagonist navigating society's margins
Structured around life and society as it rebuilds (or fails to) after a collapse has already occurred
Internalized action; mental and emotional states drive plot more than external events
Interstellar conflict, galactic politics, and large-scale adventure as the structural scope
Retrofuturism built on 19th-century steam and mechanical technology as the dominant aesthetic
Structural retelling and investigation of real-world criminal events
A world structured as an ideal or 'perfect' society, often used to critique the present
